questions.question: What is the main purpose of checking the stochastic model in time series analysis?
options: A) To identify patterns in the data
B) To detect overfitting and ensure the model is not too complex
questions.explanation: Checking the stochastic model ensures the model is not too complex and not overfitting the data.

questions.question: What does the term 'overfitting' refer to in the context of time series analysis?
options: A) When the model is too simple and fails to capture patterns
B) When the model is too complex and fits the noise in the data
C) When the model is too slow to capture trends
D) When the model is too fast to capture trends
questions.explanation: Overfitting occurs when a model is too complex and fits the noise in the data, rather than the underlying patterns.

questions.question: What is the main goal of model diagnostic checking in time series analysis?
options: A) To compare models and choose the best one
B) To detect overfitting and ensure the model is not too complex
D) To identify patterns in the data
questions.explanation: The main goal of model diagnostic checking is to detect overfitting and ensure the model is not too complex.

questions.question: What is the term for checking the assumptions of a time series model?
questions.explanation: Model checking refers to the process of checking the assumptions of a time series model.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a diagnostic check in time series analysis?
questions.explanation: All of the above options (residual analysis, ACF, and PACF) are examples of diagnostic checks in time series analysis.
